title: This Indicator Tells You EXACTLY When a New Trend Starts!
thesis_bullets: This indicator is the best trend indicator ever created on Trading View.
I will teach you a high win rate trading strategy based on this indicator.
All right, here we click on indicators and we search TAC directional movement.
this one which is an editor specs indicator and written by point coders TASC.
All right, this is the indicator and as you can see it's added here below the chart in the form of an oscillator.
You can see that we have a line here that is moving above and below the center line which is zero.
This indicator was originally developed by John Eler's many years ago and now we have it here on trading view platform.
This indicator is built based on hand function.
Hand function is used for signal processing and because of this it is very suitable for financial markets to identify signals of momentum changes or trend changes in the market.
By the help of this function we have more accurate and realistic result based on the changes that are really happening in the market.

hypotheses.statement: BTC continuation edge appears when signal alignment persists on 1h.
hypotheses.rationale: Research and indicator mentions converge on trend-continuation logic with confirmation filters.
hypotheses.falsifiable_test: Backtest BTC on 1h: require >=55% win rate and positive expectancy over 200+ trades; reject if unmet.

candidate_signals.description: Enter only when primary trend signal and confirmation filter agree on bar close.

strategy_heuristics_used: Every hypothesis should include explicit regime assumptions and invalidate conditions.
Separate signal logic from execution logic to reduce confounded performance attribution.
Require risk gating concepts before scaling complexity in entry or exit rules.
automation_heuristics_used: Keep human approval checkpoints for high-impact automation and deployment transitions.
Enforce deterministic session gating before any live-capable execution pathway is enabled.
Invest first in telemetry for risk, latency, and execution drift before adding new automation features.
